heading: Boston Harbor Islands National Recreation Area
label: 460kkk Boston Harbor Islands National Recreation Area

children.text: to preserve for public use and enjoyment the lands and waters that comprise the Boston Harbor Islands National Recreation Area;

to manage the recreation area in partnership with the private sector, the Commonwealth of Massachusetts, municipalities surrounding Massachusetts and Cape Cod Bays, the Thompson Island Outward Bound Education Center, and Trustees of Reservations, and with historical, business, cultural, civic, recreational and tourism organizations;
to improve access to the Boston Harbor Islands through the use of public water transportation; and

to provide education and visitor information programs to increase public understanding of and appreciation for the natural and cultural resources of the Boston Harbor Islands, including the history of Native American use and involvement.
the term “recreation area” means the Boston Harbor Islands National Recreation Area established by subsection (c); and
the term “Secretary” means the Secretary of the Interior.

children.heading: Boston Harbor Islands National Recreation Area
children.label: c Boston Harbor Islands National Recreation Area

children.heading: Establishment
children.label: 1 Establishment
children.text: In order to preserve for the benefit and inspiration of the people of the United States as a national recreation area certain lands located in Massachusetts Bay, there is established as a unit of the National Park System the Boston Harbor Islands National Recreation Area.

children.label: A
children.text: The recreation area shall be comprised of the lands, waters, and submerged lands generally depicted on the map entitled “Proposed Boston Harbor Islands NRA”, numbered BOHA 80,002, and dated September 1996. Such map shall be on file and available for public inspection in the appropriate offices of the National Park Service. After advising the Committee on Resources of the House of Representatives and the Committee on Energy and Natural Resources of the Senate, in writing, the Secretary may make minor revisions of the boundaries of the recreation area when necessary by publication of a revised drawing or other boundary description in the Federal Register.

children.label: i
children.text: The areas depicted on the map referenced in subparagraph (A).

children.label: ii
children.text: Landside points required for access, visitor services, and administration in the city of Boston along its Harborwalk and at Long Wharf, Fan Pier, John F. Kennedy Library, and the Custom House; Charlestown Navy Yard; Old Northern Avenue Bridge; the city of Quincy at Squantum Point/Marina Bay, the Fore River Shipyard, and Town River; the Town of Hingham at Hewitt’s Cove; the Town of Hull; the city of Salem at Salem National Historic Site; and the city of Lynn at the Heritage State Park.

children.heading: Land acquisition
children.label: 3 Land acquisition
children.text: Notwithstanding subsection (h), the Secretary is authorized to acquire, in partnership with other entities, a less than fee interest in lands at Thompson Island within the recreation area. The Secretary may acquire the lands only by donation, purchase with donated or appropriated funds, or by exchange.

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: The recreation area shall be administered in partnership by the Secretary, the Commonwealth of Massachusetts, City of Boston and its applicable subdivisions and others in accordance with the provisions of law generally applicable to units of the National Park System, including the Act entitled “An Act to establish a National Park Service, and for other purposes”, approved (; , 2, 3, and 4), and the Act of (; ) as amended and supplemented and in accordance with the integrated management plan specified in subsection (f).August 25, 1916August 21, 193539 Stat. 53516 U.S.C. 1149 Stat. 66616 U.S.C. 461–4671See References in Text note below.1

children.heading: State and local jurisdiction
children.label: 2 State and local jurisdiction
children.text: Nothing in this section shall be construed to diminish, enlarge, or modify any right of the Commonwealth of Massachusetts or any political subdivision thereof, to exercise civil and criminal jurisdiction or to carry out State laws, rules, and regulations within the recreation area, including those relating to fish and wildlife, or to tax persons, corporations, franchises, or private property on the lands and waters included in the recreation area.

children.label: i
children.text: the Commonwealth of Massachusetts;

children.label: ii
children.text: a political subdivision of the Commonwealth of Massachusetts; or

children.label: iii
children.text: any other entity that is a member of the Boston Harbor Islands Partnership described in subsection (e)(2).

children.label: i
children.text: a cooperative management agreement to acquire from, and provide to, the eligible entity goods and services for the cooperative management of land within the recreation area; and

children.label: ii
children.text: notwithstanding , a cooperative agreement for the construction of recreation area facilities on land owned by an eligible entity for purposes consistent with the management plan under subsection (f).section 6305 of title 31

children.label: i
children.text: appropriations for carrying out the purposes of the agreement are available; and

children.label: ii
children.text: the agreement is in the best interests of the United States.

children.heading: Construction of facilities on non-Federal lands
children.label: 4 Construction of facilities on non-Federal lands
children.text: In order to facilitate the administration of the recreation area, the Secretary is authorized, subject to the appropriation of necessary funds in advance, to construct essential administrative or visitor use facilities on non-Federal public lands within the recreation area. Such facilities and the use thereof shall be in conformance with applicable plans.

children.heading: Other property, funds, and services
children.label: 5 Other property, funds, and services
children.text: The Secretary may accept and use donated funds, property, and services to carry out this section.

children.heading: Relationship of recreation area to Boston-Logan International Airport
children.label: 6 Relationship of recreation area to Boston-Logan International Airport
children.text: With respect to the recreation area, the present and future maintenance, operation, improvement and use of Boston-Logan International Airport and associated flight patterns from time to time in effect shall not be deemed to constitute the use of publicly owned land of a public park, recreation area, or other resource within the meaning of , and shall not be deemed to have a significant effect on natural, scenic, and recreation assets within the meaning of .section 303(c) of title 49section 47101(h)(2) of title 49

children.heading: Management in accordance with integrated management plan
children.label: 7 Management in accordance with integrated management plan
children.text: The Secretary shall preserve, interpret, manage, and provide educational and recreational uses for the recreation area, in consultation with the owners and managers of lands in the recreation area, in accordance with the integrated management plan.

children.heading: Boston Harbor Islands Partnership establishment
children.label: e Boston Harbor Islands Partnership establishment

children.heading: Establishment
children.label: 1 Establishment
children.text: There is hereby established the Boston Harbor Islands Partnership whose purpose shall be to coordinate the activities of Federal, State, and local authorities and the private sector in the development and implementation of an integrated resource management plan for the recreation area.

children.label: A
children.text: One individual, appointed by the Secretary, to represent the National Park Service.

children.label: B
children.text: One individual, appointed by the Commandant of the Coast Guard..2So in original.2

children.label: C
children.text: Two individuals, appointed by the Secretary, after consideration of recommendations by the Governor of Massachusetts, to represent the Department of Environmental Management and the Metropolitan District Commission.

children.label: D
children.text: One individual, appointed by the Secretary, after consideration of recommendations by the Chair, to represent the Massachusetts Port Authority.

children.label: E
children.text: One individual, appointed by the Secretary, after consideration of recommendations by the Chair, to represent the Massachusetts Water Resources Authority.

children.label: F
children.text: One individual, appointed by the Secretary, after consideration of recommendations by the Mayor of Boston, to represent the Office of Environmental Services of the City of Boston.

children.label: G
children.text: One individual, appointed by the Secretary, after consideration of recommendations by the Chair, to represent the Boston Redevelopment Authority.

children.label: H
children.text: One individual, appointed by the Secretary, after consideration of recommendations of the President of the Thompson Island Outward Bound Education Center, to represent the Center.

children.label: I
children.text: One individual, appointed by the Secretary, after consideration of recommendations of the Chair, to represent the Trustees of Reservations.

children.label: J
children.text: One individual, appointed by the Secretary, after consideration of recommendations of the President of the Island Alliance, to represent the Alliance, a nonprofit organization whose sole purpose is to provide financial support for the Boston Harbor Islands National Recreation Area.

children.label: K
children.text: Two individuals, appointed by the Secretary, to represent the Boston Harbor Islands Advisory Council, established in subsection (g).

children.label: A
children.text: Members of the Partnership shall serve for terms of three years. Any member may be reappointed for one additional 3-year term.

children.label: B
children.text: The Secretary shall appoint the first members of the Partnership within 30 days after the date on which the Secretary has received all of the recommendations for appointment pursuant to subparagraphs (C), (D), (E), (F), (G), (H), (I), and (J) of paragraph (2).

children.label: C
children.text: A member may serve after the expiration of his or her term until a successor has been appointed.

children.heading: Compensation
children.label: 4 Compensation
children.text: Members of the Partnership shall serve without pay, but while away from their homes or regular places of business in the performance of services for the Partnership, members shall be allowed travel expenses, including per diem in lieu of subsistence, in the same manner as persons employed intermittently in the Government service are allowed expenses under .section 5703 of title 5

children.heading: Election of officers
children.label: 5 Election of officers
children.text: The Partnership shall elect one of its members as Chairperson and one as Vice Chairperson. The term of office of the Chairperson and Vice Chairperson shall be one year. The Vice Chairperson shall serve as chairperson in the absence of the Chairperson.

children.heading: Vacancy
children.label: 6 Vacancy
children.text: Any vacancy on the Partnership shall be filled in the same manner in which the original appointment was made.

children.heading: Meetings
children.label: 7 Meetings
children.text: The Partnership shall meet at the call of the Chairperson or a majority of its members.

children.heading: Quorum
children.label: 8 Quorum
children.text: A majority of the Partnership shall constitute a quorum.

children.heading: Staff of the Partnership
children.label: 9 Staff of the Partnership
children.text: The Secretary shall provide the Partnership with such staff and technical assistance as the Secretary, after consultation with the Partnership, considers appropriate to enable the Partnership to carry out its duties. The Secretary may accept the services of personnel detailed from the Commonwealth of Massachusetts, any political subdivision of the Commonwealth or any entity represented on the Partnership.

children.heading: Hearings
children.label: 10 Hearings
children.text: The Partnership may hold such hearings, sit and act at such times and places, take such testimony, and receive such evidence as the Partnership may deem appropriate.

children.heading: Donations
children.label: 11 Donations
children.text: Notwithstanding any other provision of law, the Partnership may seek and accept donations of funds, property, or services from individuals, foundations, corporations, and other private and public entities for the purpose of carrying out this section.

children.heading: Use of funds to obtain money
children.label: 12 Use of funds to obtain money
children.text: The Partnership may use its funds to obtain money from any source under any program or law requiring the recipient of such money to make a contribution in order to receive such money.

children.heading: Mails
children.label: 13 Mails
children.text: The Partnership may use the United States mails in the same manner and upon the same conditions as other departments and agencies of the United States.

children.heading: Obtaining property
children.label: 14 Obtaining property
children.text: The Partnership may obtain by purchase, rental, donation, or otherwise, such property, facilities, and services as may be needed to carry out its duties, except that the Partnership may not acquire any real property or interest in real property.

children.heading: Cooperative agreements
children.label: 15 Cooperative agreements
children.text: For purposes of carrying out the plan described in subsection (f), the Partnership may enter into cooperative agreements with the Commonwealth of Massachusetts, any political subdivision thereof, or with any organization or person.

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: Within three years after , the Partnership shall submit to the Secretary a management plan for the recreation area to be developed and implemented by the Partnership.November 12, 1996

children.label: i
children.text: A plan to finance and support the public improvements and services recommended in the plan, including allocation of non-Federal matching requirements set forth in subsection (h)(2) and a delineation of private-sector roles and responsibilities.

children.label: ii
children.text: A program for the coordination and consolidation, to the extent feasible, of activities that may be carried out by Federal, State, and local agencies having jurisdiction over land and waters within the recreation area, including planning and regulatory responsibilities.

children.label: i
children.text: Enhancing public outdoor recreational opportunities in the recreation area.

children.label: ii
children.text: Conserving, protecting, and maintaining the scenic, historical, cultural, natural and scientific values of the islands.

children.label: iii
children.text: Developing educational opportunities in the recreation area.

children.label: iv
children.text: Enhancing public access to the Islands, including development of transportation networks.

children.label: v
children.text: Identifying potential sources of revenue from programs or activities carried out within the recreation area.

children.label: vi
children.text: Protecting and preserving Native American burial grounds connected with the King Philip’s War internment period and other periods.

children.label: C
children.text: A policy statement that recognizes existing economic activities within the recreation area.

children.label: A
children.text: consult on a regular basis with appropriate officials of any local government or Federal or State agency which has jurisdiction over lands and waters within the recreation area;

children.label: B
children.text: consult with interested conservation, business, professional, and citizen organizations; and

children.label: C
children.text: conduct public hearings or meetings for the purposes of providing interested persons with the opportunity to testify with respect to matters to be addressed by the plan.

children.label: i
children.text: The adequacy of public participation.

children.label: ii
children.text: Assurances of plan implementation from State and local officials.

children.label: iii
children.text: The adequacy of regulatory and financial tools that are in place to implement the plan.

children.label: B
children.text: If the Secretary disapproves the plan, the Secretary shall within 60 days after the date of such disapproval, advise the Partnership in writing of the reasons therefore, together with recommendations for revision. Within 90 days of receipt of such notice of disapproval, the Partnership shall revise and resubmit the plan to the Secretary who shall approve or disapprove the revision within 60 days.

children.heading: Interim program
children.label: 5 Interim program
children.text: Prior to adoption of the Partnership’s plan, the Secretary and the Partnership shall assist the owners and managers of lands and waters within the recreation area to ensure that existing programs, services, and activities that promote the purposes of this section are supported.

children.heading: Boston Harbor Islands Advisory Council
children.label: g Boston Harbor Islands Advisory Council

children.heading: Establishment
children.label: 1 Establishment
children.text: The Secretary, acting through the Director of the National Park Service, shall establish an advisory committee to be known as the Boston Harbor Islands Advisory Council. The purpose of the Advisory Council shall be to represent various groups with interests in the recreation area and make recommendations to the Boston Harbor Islands Partnership on issues related to the development and implementation of the integrated resource management plan developed under subsection (f). The Advisory Council is encouraged to establish committees relating to specific recreation area management issues, including (but not limited to) education, tourism, transportation, natural resources, cultural and historic resources, and revenue-raising activities. Participation on any such committee shall not be limited to members of the Advisory Council.

children.heading: Membership
children.label: 2 Membership
children.text: The Advisory Council shall consist of not fewer than 18 individuals, to be appointed by the Secretary, acting through the Director of the National Park Service. The Secretary shall appoint no fewer than three individuals to represent each of the following categories of entities: municipalities; educational and cultural institutions; environmental organizations; business and commercial entities, including those related to transportation, tourism and the maritime industry; and Boston Harbor-related advocacy organizations; and organizations representing Native American interests.

children.heading: Procedures
children.label: 3 Procedures
children.text: Each meeting of the Advisory Council and its committees shall be open to the public.

children.heading: Chapter 10 of title 5
children.label: 4 Chapter 10 of title 5
children.text: The provisions of are hereby waived with respect to the Advisory Council.section 1013 of title 5

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: There are authorized to be appropriated such sums as may be necessary to carry out this section, provided that no funds may be appropriated for land acquisition.

children.heading: Matching requirement
children.label: 2 Matching requirement
children.text: Amounts appropriated in any fiscal year to carry out this section may only be expended on a matching basis in a ratio of at least three non-Federal dollars to every Federal dollar. The non-Federal share of the match may be in the form of cash, services, or in-kind contributions, fairly valued.
